messages.content: Welcome. To start, what attracted you to this customer support role?
I like solving problems for people and I stay calm when someone is frustrated. In my last job I worked the front desk, so I handled complaints every day and learned to listen first before responding.
Tell me about a specific time you turned an unhappy customer into a satisfied one.

A customer was upset that an order arrived late. I apologized without making excuses, checked the tracking, explained exactly what went wrong, and arranged a replacement with faster shipping. I followed up two days later to confirm it arrived, and he was satisfied in the end.
This role uses a ticketing system and expects clear written updates. How do you keep your communication clear when the queue is heavy?

I write short, plain updates and confirm the next step at the end of every message. When the queue is heavy I group similar tickets and answer the time-sensitive ones first. I would rather send a quick honest status than leave someone waiting in silence.
What part of support do you find most difficult, and how do you handle it?

Saying no to a request I cannot fulfill is hard for me. I handle it by being honest about the limit, explaining why, and offering the closest alternative I can. I have learned that a clear no with a reason lands better than a vague maybe.
Thank you. That concludes the questions for this session.
